summary: The Meson/Ninja build failed during compilation of the SIMD moments code due to an explicit template instantiation/signature mismatch between xsimd NEON types, causing the compiler to reject the instantiation and stop the build.
root_cause: An explicit template instantiation uses xsimd::neon64 while the corresponding function template/definition expects xsimd::neon, producing a signature mismatch and causing the compiler error.

evidence.quote: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: Shows the compiler rejected an explicit template instantiation (compilation error).

evidence.quote: could not match 'Moments (xsimd::neon64, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int) noexcept' against 'Moments (xsimd::neon, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int)'
evidence.reason: Directly indicates the signature/type mismatch between xsimd::neon64 and xsimd::neon that caused the instantiation failure.
Points to the source location of the explicit template instantiation implicated in the failure.
Identifies the object file whose compilation failed due to the instantiation error.
Shows the build system stopped because the compilation subcommand failed.
Indicates the overall build step failed with a nonzero exit status.

suggested_fix: Make the explicit instantiation in pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp (around line 255) match the actual function template type: either change the instantiation to use xsimd::neon (to match the definitions) or update the function/template definitions to use xsimd::neon64. Also verify the build configuration selects the intended NEON variant so all instantiation points are consistent.
